reasoning: Each thread performs a serial reduction over one row, issuing 1024 dependent `acc += in[...]` operations, so the loop has a long dependency chain with little instruction-level parallelism. Across a warp, threads read different rows at the same column index, which are separated by `cols=1024` floats (4096 bytes), so loads are poorly coalesced; this typically yields low effective DRAM utilization rather than bandwidth saturation, matching a latency-bound kernel rather than memory-bound on an A10G.
